Best Fish Oil for Dogs 2026: Omega-3 Supplements Reviewed

Published on March 4, 2026

⚠️ Affiliate Disclosure: This post may contain affiliate links. We earn a commission if you make a purchase, at no extra cost to you.

Quick Summary

After testing 15+ fish oil supplements, here are our top picks for 2026:

🏆 Top Pick: Nordic Naturals Omega-3 Pet

Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 4.8/5

Best for: Skin, coat, and joint health

Price: $27.95 for 8oz

Benefits of Fish Oil for Dogs

Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA) provide:

  • ✅ Healthier skin and coat - reduces shedding and itching
  • ✅ Joint health - reduces inflammation and arthritis pain
  • ✅ Heart health - supports cardiovascular function
  • ✅ Brain function - especially important for puppies and seniors
  • ✅ Immune support - helps fight inflammation

Top 10 Fish Oil Supplements

1. Nordic Naturals Omega-3 Pet - Best Overall

Pros: Wild-caught fish, third-party tested, great taste, no fishy burps

Cons: Premium price, liquid form only

Check Price on Amazon →

2. Grizzly Salmon Oil - Best Value

Pros: Wild Alaskan salmon, pump dispenser, affordable

Cons: Strong fishy smell, some dogs may not like taste

Check Price on Amazon →

3. Zesty Paws Omega Bites - Best Tasty

Pros: Chewable form, dogs love the taste, includes other supplements

Cons: Lower EPA/DHA per serving, contains fillers

Check Price on Amazon →

4. Nutramax Welactin - Best for Potency

Pros: High concentration, molecularly distilled, easy to dose

Cons: Expensive, small bottle size

Check Price on Amazon →

5. Vet’s Best Omega-3 - Best Budget

Pros: Affordable, includes vitamin E, easy to find

Cons: Lower quality sourcing, moderate potency

Check Price on Amazon →

Dosage Guide

Dog WeightDaily EPA+DHA
Under 10 lbs100-250 mg
10-25 lbs250-500 mg
25-50 lbs500-1000 mg
50-100 lbs1000-2000 mg
Over 100 lbs2000+ mg

Always consult your vet before starting supplements.

What to Look For

Quality Indicators

  • ✅ Wild-caught fish (better than farmed)
  • ✅ Third-party tested for purity
  • ✅ Molecularly distilled (removes contaminants)
  • ✅ Clear EPA/DHA amounts on label
  • ✅ Added vitamin E (prevents oxidation)

Red Flags

  • ❌ Vague sourcing (“fish oil” without specifics)
  • ❌ No third-party testing
  • ❌ Artificial colors or flavors
  • ❌ Excessive fillers

FAQ

How long until I see results?

Most dogs show improvement in 4-8 weeks. Skin and coat improvements may take 8-12 weeks.

Can I give too much fish oil?

Yes. Excessive fish oil can cause diarrhea, weight gain, or affect blood clotting. Follow dosage guidelines.

Liquid vs capsules - which is better?

Liquid is easier to dose and mix with food. Capsules are more convenient for travel. Choose based on your dog’s preference.
